database_info.name: Interactive Display & Digital Signage Selection Theory
database_info.purpose: Engineering design tool for interactive display and digital signage selection

1_performance_vs_ultra_series.principle: Series determines feature level, touch capability, and smart meeting features

comparison.best_for: Education, training rooms, basic meeting rooms

comparison.key_features: Auto framing, speaker tracking, NFC mirroring, advanced whiteboard, multi-screen collaboration
comparison.best_for: Executive boardrooms, premium conference rooms, smart offices

2_screen_size_selection.principle: Screen size must ensure content is readable from the furthest viewer

guide.best_for: Small meeting room, huddle space, classroom (up to 20 students)
Large conference room, lecture hall, boardroom
Large boardroom, auditorium, executive presentation room

2_screen_size_selection.formula: Max viewing distance ≈ screen diagonal (inches) × 0.1 meters. For text-heavy content, reduce by 30%.

3_ops_module_selection.principle: OPS module adds Windows PC capability inside the IFP panel

options.best_for: Standard Office (Teams/Zoom, Office 365, web browsing, presentations)
Basic whiteboard, screen sharing from laptops/phones, signage

3_ops_module_selection.decision: If users need Windows applications (Teams, Zoom desktop, Office, custom software) → OPS module required. If only whiteboard + wireless presentation → Android is sufficient.

4_wireless_presentation.principle: Wireless screen sharing eliminates cable clutter and enables BYOD

methods.best_for: BYOD — any device can connect without dongle
Instant one-tap mirroring from Android phones
Reliable, zero-latency for video playback, demos

5_digital_signage_selection.principle: Signage player + display combination for content delivery

player_box.function: Android media player box → HDMI to any display
player_box.best_for: Adding signage capability to existing monitors/TVs

freestanding_display.best_for: Retail, hotel lobby, reception, wayfinding, menu boards

6_mounting_and_brackets.principle: Bracket must match IFP size and weight, and provide correct viewing height

brackets.best_for: Clean flush look, no height adjustment needed

mounting_height.for_touch_use: Bottom edge at 700-900mm above floor for comfortable touch interaction while standing
mounting_height.ada: Ensure touch area is reachable from wheelchair (max 1220mm/48 inches AFF)

6_mounting_and_brackets.wall_requirement: Reinforced wall required. IFP weight: 65"≈40kg, 75"≈55kg, 86"≈70kg, 98"≈100kg. Use appropriate anchors.

7_network_and_power.principle: IFP requires power + network for full functionality

requirements.network: Wired LAN (Cat5e/Cat6) recommended for reliable firmware updates and content delivery. Wi-Fi available as backup.
requirements.hdmi_cable: High-speed HDMI 2.0 for 4K@60Hz from external sources. Cable included (3m typical).
requirements.usb_touch: USB cable from OPS or external laptop to IFP for touch-back functionality (3m cable included).
requirements.conduit: Plan conduit behind IFP location for power + LAN + HDMI during construction phase.

8_room_integration.principle: IFP integrates with room AV for complete meeting experience

9_content_management_signage.principle: Centralized content management for multi-display signage deployments

10_compliance.principle: Interactive displays must meet safety, EMC, and accessibility standards

standards.scope: Accessibility: touch area reachable height, wheelchair users
Low blue light for eye care in education settings
